data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't see it. This is a classic MSBuild issue: **glob-based implicit `<Compile>` items are evaluated at evaluation time**, before any targets run, so the generated file is never included in compilation.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group inside the target, and register it in `FileWrites` for proper clean support.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add to Compile so the compiler sees it (globs run at evaluation time, before this target) -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and writes the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's glob-based implicit `Compile` items are evaluated at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also register it in `FileWrites` for clean support.

Let me use the including-generated-files skill since it's exactly relevant.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add to Compile so the compiler sees it (globs run at evaluation time, before this target) -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** MSBuild's implicit `<Compile>` glob (`**/*.cs`) is evaluated at **evaluation time**, before any targets execute. The `GenerateSampleCode` target creates the file during **execution time**, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to explicitly include the generated file in `Compile` and register it in `FileWrites` (so `dotnet clean` removes it):

```xml
<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
<FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
```
